who_inn_similarity_review.interpretation:
Product name 'Synthetic Synteromycin Product' has 42.86% similarity to INN 'synteromycin'. Rule result: pass. Evaluated against naming guidance: WHO AWaRe and GLASS Policy Stack. AWaRe Source of Truth.AWaRe Source of Truth

WHO AWaRe is the authoritative source for Access, Watch, and Reserve assignment. Production policy decisions must not rely on synthetic or hand-maintained substitute lists. who aware glass antimicrobial-resistance...
who_inn_similarity_review.rule_consequence: No blocking INN naming issue was identified.

amr_stewardship_review.stewardship_rule_application: Restricted authorization is recommended to preserve last-resort effectiveness.
findings: MCP AWaRe reference resolved synteromycin as Unknown.
MCP antimicrobial similarity returned stewardship flag not_applicable.
RxNorm snapshot had no normalization entry for synteromycin; used dossier product identity as provided.
WHO AWaRe snapshot had no entry for synteromycin; fell back to dossier policy_signals.
WHO GLASS snapshot had no entry for synteromycin; fell back to dossier policy_signals.
Chemistry snapshot had no entry for synteromycin; fell back to dossier policy_signals for Watch similarity.

report_payload.email_body:
Complete dossier E2E E2E-HUM-NEW-INNOV-AMR-SYNT-0001

Submission Summary
- Dossier Id: E2E-HUM-NEW-INNOV-AMR-SYNT-0001
- Submission Type: pre_market_authorization
- Review Type: generic
- Applicant: Synthetic Applicant 858
- Product Name: Synthetic Synteromycin Product
- Active Ingredient: synteromycin
- Dosage Form: oral suspension
- Strength: 250 mg/5 mL
- Jurisdiction: Rwanda
- Review Pathway: standard

Administrative Review Outcome
- Status: administratively_complete
- No administrative violations were detected from the available dossier evidence.

Dossier Structure Review Outcome
- Status: mapped
- No structural dossier violations were detected.

Applicable Rules And Requirements
- Administrative completeness checklist
- Structural dossier completeness rules
- WHO INN similarity rule
- Section adequacy and evidence sufficiency rules
- Generic review packaging and patient-information comparison against innovator reference when provided
- WHO AWaRe stewardship rules

WHO INN Similarity Review
- Proposed Name: Synthetic Synteromycin Product
- Who Inn: synteromycin
- Similarity Index: 0.4286
- Threshold: 0.7
